title: React Conditional Rendering Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of if...else, ternary operators, and logical && syntax in React.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the most common way to conditionally include an element in your React UI with a single true condition?
What is the return type if you don't want a component to render anything at all?
Which of the following is correct syntax for a ternary operator in JSX?
Can you use a basic 'if' statement inside a JSX expression returning another JSX element?
options: No, you must use it before the return statement or use a ternary/logical operator.
Yes, but only in older versions of React.
Only if you wrap the if in curly braces {}

questions.question: What happens if a logical && condition evaluates to false (e.g., false && <E />)?
options: React will render the literal string 'false'.
React will ignore it and render nothing for that part.
React will render an empty div.
